response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and applications software engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and some embedded/low-latency systems, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and deep-learning workload analysis. The resume shows no experience in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, or DNN training/inference optimization, and lacks the advanced engineering/CS degree (or equivalent specialized experience) the role expects. The skill profile is essentially orthogonal to the hardware-architecture requirements.
strengths: Deep, long software engineering career with low-latency systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Some embedded and C/C++ exposure (robotic arms, National Instruments LabWindows/CVI)
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning workload optimization background
Lacks the relevant advanced engineering/CS degree or equivalent specialized hardware experience

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator / hardware architecture experience
No SoC/IP modeling or HW-SW co-design
